entries.summary: QA audit complete. ClipSanitizer codebase reviewed with 14 candidate findings generated, 6 confirmed after reviewer validation, and 2 HIGH findings passed through inline critic challenge. One HIGH upheld (README CHARMAP count), one HIGH refined to MEDIUM (smoke test incomplete). Core security model (Electron hardening) passed cleanly — no security findings. Primary failure mode: documentation drift after feature expansion.
lessons_learned: Documentation drift is the dominant AI failure mode — clinical units, full-width ASCII, and transliteration entries were added to code but never documented in README
Smoke tests that only check a subset of return shape give false-pass confidence — always assert complete contract including warnings/defaults
Lockfile staleness (version drift after v1.0.1 bump) is a simple maintenance gap easily caught by pre-commit hooks
NFKC vs NFKD is a meaningful distinction — both are Unicode normalization forms but produce different results for some characters
Electron sandbox:true + contextBridge is correctly implemented with no Node API leakage — security posture is strong
